patterns_discovered.description: Implements both access and refresh token generation, verification, and error handling with distinct secrets and expiry times
Uses TypeScript interfaces to extend Express Request type with authenticated user context
Provides three authentication middleware variants: required (authMiddleware), optional (optionalAuthMiddleware), and role/permission-based (requireRoles, requirePermissions)
Distinguishes between JWT library errors (TokenExpiredError, JsonWebTokenError) and application-level exceptions (AppException)
Standardized extraction and validation of Bearer tokens from Authorization headers
Implements SECURITY_MODEL.md specification with claim validation, token ID tracking for revocation, and permission caching

gotchas_discovered.description: Required claims (sub, org, role) are validated after jwt.verify() to ensure token structure compliance beyond JWT signature verification
optionalAuthMiddleware intentionally suppresses errors to allow requests without valid tokens, which could mask real issues if not properly documented

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Separate Secrets for Access and Refresh Tokens
gotchas_discovered.description: Uses env.JWT_SECRET for access tokens but env.JWT_REFRESH_SECRET for refresh tokens, requiring both to be configured
Middleware checks if user exists before checking role/permission, so authMiddleware must be applied first
JWT algorithm is hardcoded to HS256 in verification, which must match the algorithm used during token generation

approach_outcome.implementation_approach: Created a comprehensive JWT authentication middleware module following security best practices and SECURITY_MODEL.md specification
key_accomplishments: Implemented required and optional authentication middleware
Created role-based and permission-based authorization middleware
Implemented both access and refresh token generation and verification
Provided utility functions for common authentication operations
Added extensive JSDoc documentation with examples

recommendations.recommendation: Add token revocation mechanism by implementing token ID (jti) checking against a blacklist/denylist database
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation tracks token IDs but doesn't prevent revoked tokens from being used

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive unit tests for all middleware functions covering success paths, error cases, and edge cases
recommendations.rationale: No test file provided; authentication middleware requires thorough testing

recommendations.recommendation: Document required environment variables (JWT_SECRET, JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRY, JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRY) with examples
recommendations.rationale: Module depends on multiple env variables that must be properly configured

recommendations.recommendation: Standardize error response format for all authentication failures in the error handling middleware
recommendations.rationale: Module throws AppException but doesn't specify how it should be formatted in HTTP responses

recommendations.recommendation: Consider caching permission validation results to avoid repeated checks for the same user
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation validates permissions on every request even though payload is cached

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration example showing complete auth flow (login, token storage, protected routes, refresh flow)
recommendations.rationale: Individual functions are well-documented but complete usage pattern is not shown
